description: Complete the APIdown.net platform end-to-end: MVP polish, dashboard visuals, SDKs, auth, admin, billing, alerting, SEO, and operational readiness

userStories.title: Remove temporary error handling from homepage load function
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the homepage load function to use proper error handling instead of the debug try/catch that returns empty arrays, so that SvelteKit error pages work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove try/catch wrapper from +page.server.js load function
Supabase query errors throw properly so SvelteKit +error.svelte renders
Page still loads successfully when Supabase is reachable
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add favicon and meta assets
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a proper favicon and Open Graph meta tags so the site looks professional in browser tabs and social shares.
acceptanceCriteria: Add favicon.svg or favicon.png to frontend/static/
Add OG image (1200x630) to frontend/static/
Update app.html or +layout.svelte with og:image, og:title, og:description meta tags
favicon.ico request no longer returns 500
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add nav links and footer to layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want persistent navigation and a footer so I can easily navigate the site.
acceptanceCriteria: Update +layout.svelte to include a header with nav links: Home, Incidents, Docs, Get API Key
Add footer with: 'Built by Pearson Media LLC', links to GitHub, API status endpoint
Nav highlights current route using $page.url.pathname
Mobile-responsive: hamburger menu on small screens
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add latency sparklines to homepage status cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a small 24-hour latency sparkline on each API card on the homepage so I can spot trends at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage load function fetches last 24h of signals_1min data per API (grouped into ~24 hourly buckets)
StatusCard component renders a small inline SVG sparkline (avg latency per hour)
Sparkline is ~80px wide, fits inside the card without layout changes
APIs with no data show a flat gray line or placeholder
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add 24-hour latency chart to API detail page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a full-width latency chart on the API detail page showing P50 and P95 latency over the last 24 hours.
acceptanceCriteria: New LatencyChart.svelte component in frontend/src/lib/components/
Renders P50 line (blue) and P95 line (orange) on the same chart
X-axis shows time labels (every 4 hours), Y-axis shows milliseconds
Chart is pure SVG, no external chart library
Renders on the API detail page between metrics row and incident history
Empty state shows 'No latency data yet' message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add regional breakdown to API detail page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a per-region status breakdown on the API detail page so I can tell if degradation is regional or global.
acceptanceCriteria: API detail page load function groups signals_1min data by region for last 1 hour
New RegionBreakdown.svelte component shows each region with its own error rate and avg latency
Regions displayed as a small grid of cards below the latency chart
Each region card shows: region name, signal count, error rate %, avg latency ms
Color-coded: green (<5% errors), yellow (5-20%), red (>20%)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add 90-day uptime bar to API detail page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a visual 90-day uptime bar (like GitHub status) on the API detail page showing daily status.
acceptanceCriteria: API detail page load function fetches incidents for last 90 days
New UptimeBar.svelte component renders 90 thin vertical bars (one per day)
Green = no incidents, yellow = minor/major incident, red = critical or down
Hover on a bar shows tooltip with date and incident count
Shows overall uptime percentage label (already calculated, reuse uptimePercent)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add reporter count to incident cards and detail pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see how many independent reporters confirmed an incident so I can gauge its credibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Add reporter_count INTEGER column to incidents table via migration
Anomaly detector stores unique_reporters count when creating/updating incidents
Incident list and detail pages display 'X reporters' badge
Badge helps users distinguish between low-confidence and high-confidence incidents
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Used manual_reports table count during incident window instead of adding a column to incidents table

userStories.title: Add incident timeline/updates table
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want incidents to have timeline entries so users can see investigation progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Create database migration: incident_updates table with id, incident_id (FK), status, message, created_at
When anomaly detector changes incident status, insert an incident_update row
When incident is auto-resolved, insert a resolution update
RLS: public read, service_role write
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created incident_updates table migration and added timeline inserts to anomaly detector

userStories.title: Display incident timeline on incident detail page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a timeline of status updates on the incident detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: Incident detail page load function fetches incident_updates ordered by created_at
Timeline rendered as a vertical list with timestamp, status badge, and message
Status badges color-coded: investigating=yellow, identified=orange, monitoring=blue, resolved=green
Empty state: 'No updates yet'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add JSON REST API endpoint for single API status
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a public REST API that returns a single API's current status with metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /api-status/[slug] server endpoint returning single API detail
Response includes: slug, name, status, uptime_90d, p50_ms, p95_ms, active_incidents count
Returns 404 JSON for unknown slugs
Cache-Control: public, max-age=60
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Python SDK package structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Python SDK (apidown) that can be pip-installed so Python apps can contribute signal data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/sdk-python/ with pyproject.toml, README.md, src/apidown/__init__.py
Package name: apidown, version 1.0.0
Zero external dependencies (uses stdlib only: urllib, json, threading, time, atexit)
Exports: init(), shutdown(), record()
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Python SDK core with auto-patching
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want the apidown SDK to auto-patch requests and httpx libraries so my HTTP calls are automatically monitored.
acceptanceCriteria: init() patches requests.Session.send if requests is installed
init() patches httpx.Client.send if httpx is installed
record(domain, status_code, duration_ms) works for manual recording
Signals batched and flushed every 30s via daemon thread
Flush on atexit (clean shutdown)
allowlist/denylist domain filtering works
Never monitors the ingest endpoint itself
Status code 0 recorded for network errors (exceptions)
Unit tests pass for init, record, flush, domain filtering
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Auto-patches requests and httpx. Unit tests in tests/test_core.py.

userStories.title: Add users table and Supabase Auth integration
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need a users table linked to Supabase Auth so users can register and log in.
acceptanceCriteria: Create database migration: users table with id (UUID, FK to auth.users), email, display_name, tier (free/pro/team default free), created_at
RLS: users can read their own row, service_role full access
Add trigger: on auth.users insert, auto-create matching users row
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 012_create_users_table.sql with auto-trigger on auth.users insert

userStories.title: Add login and registration pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want to register and log in to APIdown so I can manage my subscriptions and API keys.
acceptanceCriteria: New route: /login with email + password form
New route: /register with email + password + display name form
Both use Supabase Auth client (supabase.auth.signInWithPassword, supabase.auth.signUp)
Redirect to /dashboard on successful auth
Show inline error messages on failure
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Combined login/register on /login page with mode toggle

userStories.title: Add auth session handling with hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need server-side session handling so protected routes can check auth state.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/hooks.server.js that reads Supabase auth session from cookies
Attach user object to event.locals.user for all server load functions
Create frontend/src/lib/auth.js helper: getUser() returns current user or null
Update +layout.server.js to pass user to all pages via data.user
Update nav: show Login/Register when logged out, show user email + Logout when logged in
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Layout server load checks sb-access-token cookie, nav shows Login/Dashboard conditionally

userStories.title: Add API key table and generation endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to generate an API key for the SDK so I can authenticate my signal submissions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create database migration: api_keys table with id, user_id (FK to users), key_hash, label, created_at, last_used_at, is_active
Key format: adn_live_<32 random hex chars>
Key stored as SHA-256 hash (never store plaintext after generation)
Add POST /v1/keys endpoint to ingest service that generates a key and returns it once
Endpoint requires valid auth token or rate-limits by IP (10 per hour)
RLS: users can read/delete their own keys, service_role full access
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 013_create_api_keys_table.sql with RLS policies

userStories.title: Add API key validation to signal ingest
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want the signal ingest endpoint to validate API keys so only registered users can submit data.
acceptanceCriteria: Signal ingest endpoint hashes the X-APIdown-Key header and checks against api_keys table
Invalid or missing key returns 401
Valid key updates last_used_at timestamp (debounced, not on every request)
Inactive keys (is_active=false) are rejected with 403
Existing signals route logic unchanged for valid keys
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Validates key_hash against api_keys table, updates last_used_at

userStories.title: Add user dashboard page
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want a /dashboard page where I can see my API keys and alert subscriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: New route: /dashboard (protected - redirects to /login if not authenticated)
Shows list of user's API keys: label, created_at, last_used_at, active status
Shows 'Generate New Key' button that calls POST /v1/keys and displays result
Copy button for each key hash prefix (show first 8 chars only, rest masked)
Deactivate/delete key buttons
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Dashboard with API keys list, create key, revoke key, logout

userStories.title: Add My Subscriptions section to user dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: Add user_id column to alert_subscriptions table (nullable, for backward compat with anonymous subs)

Unsubscribe button per subscription (calls DELETE or uses existing token-based unsubscribe)

userStories.title: Add alert preferences to user dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose between immediate alerts and hourly digest mode.

userStories.title: Add digest alert worker loop
userStories.description: As a user on digest mode, I want to receive a single hourly summary email instead of individual alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: New worker loop runs every 60 minutes
Queries alert_subscriptions where alert_mode='digest'
For each subscription, collects all undelivered incidents since last digest
Sends single email with summary of all incidents (API name, severity, status, time)
Marks alerts as delivered in alert_log to prevent re-sending
Skips if no new incidents since last digest
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: digest-worker.js runs hourly, checks for digest subscribers with recent incidents

userStories.title: Add email verification flow for alert subscriptions
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want email subscriptions to be verified before sending alerts to prevent abuse.
acceptanceCriteria: When creating an email subscription, set verified=false (change current default from true)
Generate a verification token and send a verification email with a confirm link
Add GET /v1/verify?token=xxx endpoint to ingest service that sets verified=true
Alert worker only sends to verified=true subscriptions (already the case)
Frontend subscribe form shows 'Check your email to confirm' message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GET /v1/verify endpoint, email subs default verified=false

userStories.title: Add PagerDuty alert channel
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want to receive PagerDuty alerts when an API I subscribe to has an incident.
acceptanceCriteria: Alert worker handles channel='pagerduty' subscriptions

Includes severity mapping: critical->critical, major->error, minor->warning
Resolution events sent when incident resolves
Subscription route validates PagerDuty routing key format

userStories.title: Add Discord webhook alert channel
userStories.description: As a team, I want to receive Discord alerts when an API we depend on has an incident.
acceptanceCriteria: Alert worker handles channel='discord' subscriptions

Card includes: API name, severity, status, regions, link to status page

userStories.title: Add pricing page

Pro: unlimited alerts (all channels), 10 API keys, REST API access, SLA reports
Team: everything in Pro + 10 seats, SSO, priority support

userStories.title: Enforce tier limits on alert subscriptions

acceptanceCriteria: Free tier: max 3 email-only subscriptions per user
Pro tier: unlimited subscriptions, all channels (email, slack, pagerduty, discord, teams)
Team tier: same as Pro
Anonymous (no account): max 3 email subscriptions per email address
Subscription endpoint returns 403 with clear message when limit exceeded

userStories.notes: tier-limits.js helper with TIER_LIMITS config

userStories.title: Enforce tier limits on API keys
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want API key limits enforced per tier.
acceptanceCriteria: Free tier: max 1 active API key
Pro tier: max 10 active API keys
Team tier: max 50 active API keys

Dashboard shows 'X of Y keys used' indicator

userStories.notes: Tier limits defined in tier-limits.js

userStories.title: Add synthetic signal seeder for demo data
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want to seed realistic signal and incident data so the dashboard looks useful at launch.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/seed-signals.js that generates 7 days of synthetic signal data for all APIs
Each API gets ~1000 signals/day with realistic latency distributions (normal curve around 100-500ms)
Inject 2-3 simulated incidents: elevated error rates for specific APIs during specific windows
Insert directly to Supabase signals table (bypass Redis)
After seeding signals, run refresh_signals_1min() to populate materialized view
Script is idempotent (checks for existing data before inserting)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is critical for proving the product works on launch day.

userStories.title: Seed sample incidents with timeline updates
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want pre-populated incidents so the incident history looks credible at launch.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/seed-incidents.js that creates 5-8 realistic incidents across different APIs
Mix of severities: 2 critical, 3 major, 2 minor, all resolved
Each incident has 2-4 incident_updates showing progression (investigating -> identified -> resolved)
Incidents spread over last 30 days with realistic durations (30min to 4hrs)
Affected regions vary (us-east-1, eu-west-1, ap-southeast-1)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add sitemap.xml and robots.txt

changefreq: hourly for homepage and API pages, daily for others

userStories.title: Add SEO-optimized landing page

userStories.notes: Reusable Toast.svelte with success/error/info variants, auto-dismiss, fixed position

userStories.title: Extract shared Button and Card UI components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared Button and Card components so the UI is consistent and code is DRY.
acceptanceCriteria: Create lib/components/ui/Button.svelte with variants: primary, secondary, danger, ghost
Create lib/components/ui/Card.svelte with optional header, padding, and hover props
Replace inline .btn-primary, .btn-secondary, .btn-danger-sm styles across all pages
All existing pages render identically after refactor
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Shared Button.svelte (primary/secondary/danger/ghost) and Card.svelte in lib/components/ui/

userStories.title: Add SVG accessibility labels to all chart components
userStories.description: As a screen reader user, I want chart SVGs to have proper ARIA labels and data summaries.
acceptanceCriteria: LatencyChart SVG has role='img' and aria-label describing P50/P95 data
UptimeBar segments are keyboard-navigable with arrow keys
UptimeBar tooltip content is announced via aria-live region
RegionBreakdown status uses text labels in addition to color
All color-only indicators have non-color alternatives (icons or text)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: LatencyChart has role=img aria-label; UptimeBar segments keyboard-navigable with aria-live tooltip; RegionBreakdown has text status labels

userStories.title: Add sort options to homepage API grid
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sort APIs by status (down first) or name so I can quickly find what matters.
acceptanceCriteria: Add sort toggle above the API grid: 'By Category' (default), 'By Status', 'By Name'
By Status shows down APIs first, then degraded, then operational
By Name shows flat alphabetical list without category grouping
Sort preference persists via localStorage
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Sort toggle with By Category/Status/Name, localStorage persistence

userStories.title: Add pricing page FAQ section
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a FAQ section on the pricing page to answer common billing questions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add expandable FAQ accordion below pricing cards
Include at least 5 questions: Can I downgrade?, What payment methods?, Is there a free trial?, What happens when I exceed limits?, Can I get a refund?
FAQ items expand/collapse on click with smooth animation
Accessible: uses <details>/<summary> or proper ARIA accordion pattern
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: FAQ accordion with 5 questions using native details/summary elements

userStories.title: Add mobile-responsive sticky header
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the navigation header to stay visible as I scroll so I can navigate easily.
acceptanceCriteria: Header becomes sticky (position: sticky; top: 0) on all screen sizes
Header has a slight backdrop blur and shadow when scrolled past threshold
Main content padding adjusted to account for sticky header
Mobile menu still works correctly with sticky positioning
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Header sticky with backdrop-filter blur and z-index

userStories.title: Add print styles for incident detail pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want incident detail pages to print cleanly for documentation and postmortem reports.
acceptanceCriteria: Add @media print styles to app.css
Hide navigation, footer, and interactive elements when printing
Incident timeline renders cleanly in print layout
Colors adjusted for print (dark text on white background)
Page breaks avoided in the middle of timeline entries
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Print media query in app.css hides nav/footer, adjusts colors for print

userStories.title: Add real-time connection status indicator
userStories.description: As a user, I want to know if the live update connection is active so I can trust the data is current.
acceptanceCriteria: Small 'Live' indicator in the top-right of the homepage when real-time subscription is active
Shows 'Reconnecting...' with yellow dot if connection drops
Shows 'Offline' with red dot if connection fails after retries
Supabase channel handles reconnection automatically
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Live/Reconnecting/Offline indicator based on Supabase channel status

userStories.title: Add friendly error messages for auth forms
userStories.description: As a user, I want friendly error messages on login/register forms instead of raw Supabase errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Create error message mapping in lib/auth-errors.js
Map 'Invalid login credentials' to 'Incorrect email or password. Please try again.'
Map 'User already registered' to 'An account with this email already exists. Try logging in instead.'
Map 'Email not confirmed' to 'Please check your email and click the confirmation link.'
All Supabase auth errors have user-friendly alternatives
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: auth-errors.js maps Supabase errors to friendly messages, used in login page

userStories.title: Personal API latency tracking per user API key
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want to see my app's own error rate and latency to each API (correlated by my API key), so I can compare my performance vs the global average.
acceptanceCriteria: Add RPC function get_api_window_for_user(api_id, user_id, minutes) that filters signals by reporter_hash linked to user's API keys
Add /api/[slug]/my-stats endpoint returning user-specific P50, P95, error rate alongside global stats
API detail page shows 'My App' tab/toggle (Pro+) next to global metrics with personal latency and error rate
Free users see the toggle grayed out with a Pro badge and upgrade prompt on click
Dashboard 'My Stack' summary card shows personal vs global latency for pinned APIs
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: RPC get_api_stats_for_user in migration 016, /api/[slug]/my-stats endpoint, Global/My App toggle on detail page

userStories.title: Multi-API dependency dashboard (My Stack)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pin my critical API dependencies and see a combined health overview so I can monitor my entire stack at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Add pinned_apis table (user_id, api_id, created_at) with unique constraint
Add POST/DELETE /v1/pinned-apis endpoints for pin/unpin
Dashboard shows 'My Stack' section with pinned APIs, each showing current status and P95
Combined health score: 'All Operational' / 'N APIs Degraded' / 'N APIs Down'
Pin button on API detail page (star/pin icon)
Free users can pin up to 3 APIs, Pro up to 20, Team unlimited
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: pinned_apis table in migration 016, /v1/pinned-apis endpoints, My Stack section on dashboard, star pin button on detail page

userStories.title: Downtime cost estimator on incidents
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want to set a $/minute cost per API dependency so incidents show estimated revenue impact.
acceptanceCriteria: Add cost_per_minute_cents column to pinned_apis or a new api_cost_config table
Settings UI on dashboard to set $/min per pinned API
Incident detail page shows 'Estimated Impact: $X' based on duration * cost_per_minute
Alert notifications (Pro+) include cost estimate in payload
Free users see the feature teased but locked behind Pro badge
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: cost_per_minute_cents on pinned_apis in migration 017, dashboard cost config UI, incident detail shows estimated impact

userStories.title: Locked channel indicators on alert subscription form
userStories.description: As a free user, I want to see all available alert channels with Pro-locked ones visually indicated so I understand what I'm missing and am motivated to upgrade.
acceptanceCriteria: Alert subscription form on API detail page shows all channels (email, Slack, Discord, PagerDuty, Teams, Webhook)
Free-tier channels beyond email show a lock icon and 'Pro' badge
Clicking a locked channel opens the UpgradeModal instead of the subscription form
Pro/Team users see all channels unlocked as before
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Channel chip selector with lock badges and inline upgrade prompt on API detail page

userStories.title: Historical latency comparison with date range picker
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want to compare latency across different time periods so I can identify trends and regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add date range picker component on API detail page (Pro+)
Support preset ranges: Last 24h, Last 7d, Last 30d, Custom range
Add compare mode: overlay two date ranges on the latency chart
Add RPC function get_latency_history(api_id, from, to, bucket_interval) returning time-series P50/P95
Free users see 24h only with a 'Pro' badge on the date picker
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Date range pill picker (24h/7d/30d) on API detail page, free users locked to 24h with Pro badge, hourly bucketing for 7d/30d, adaptive x-axis labels. Compare mode deferred.

userStories.title: Scheduled maintenance awareness from vendor status pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see upcoming scheduled maintenance windows for APIs so I can prepare for expected downtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend statuspage-scraper.js to fetch /api/v2/scheduled-maintenances/upcoming.json
Add scheduled_maintenances table (api_id, title, scheduled_for, scheduled_until, status, vendor_id)
API detail page shows 'Upcoming Maintenance' banner when maintenance is scheduled
Pro users receive advance maintenance alerts 24h before scheduled time
Maintenance windows excluded from uptime/SLA calculations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: scheduled_maintenances table in migration 017, scraper extended with scrapeScheduledMaintenances, amber banner on API detail page

userStories.title: Custom anomaly detection thresholds per subscription
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want to customize alert thresholds per API so I only get notified for conditions I care about.
acceptanceCriteria: Add threshold_config JSONB column to alert_subscriptions table
Subscription settings UI with sliders/inputs for: min severity (minor/major/critical), max P95 latency, min error rate %
anomaly-detector.js checks per-subscription thresholds before dispatching alerts
Default thresholds match current behavior when no custom config is set
Free users see threshold controls locked with Pro badge
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: threshold_config JSONB on alert_subscriptions (migration 020), min_severity filter in alert-worker.js, severity dropdown on subscribe form for Pro+ users, default behavior unchanged

userStories.title: Team incident annotations
userStories.description: As a Team user, I want team members to add internal notes to incidents for postmortem documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add incident_notes table (id, incident_id, user_id, content, created_at)
Incident detail page shows 'Internal Notes' section for Team tier users
Team members can add timestamped notes with markdown support
Notes are only visible to users on the same team
Add team_id column to users table and team membership validation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: incident_notes table in migration 020, Internal Notes section on incident detail page for Team tier users, client-side Supabase insert, RLS policies for read/write

userStories.title: Weekly email digest for free users
userStories.description: As a free user, I want a weekly summary email of my subscribed APIs' health so I stay engaged with the platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Add weekly-digest-worker.js running every Monday 9am UTC
Email includes: uptime % per subscribed API, incident count, notable latency changes
Free users get weekly digest, Pro users get daily digest option
Unsubscribe link in every digest email
Add digest_frequency column to users table (weekly/daily/none)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: weekly-digest-worker.js with daily/weekly logic, digest_frequency column in migration 017, dashboard toggle UI with Pro-gated daily option

userStories.title: Public embeddable status page for Pro users
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want to generate a public status page showing my pinned APIs so I can share dependency status with my own customers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /status/[username] public route showing pinned APIs with status indicators
Customizable title and description via dashboard settings
Embeddable iframe snippet and direct link provided in dashboard
Shows real-time status updates via Supabase subscription
Rate limited and cached (1 min) for public access
Only available for Pro+ users; free users see upgrade prompt
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Public /status/[slug] route with cached output (60s), dashboard settings for Pro+ (title, description, slug, enable toggle), iframe embed snippet, Powered by APIdown.net footer

userStories.title: SLA target tracking with pass/fail reporting
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want to set SLA targets per API and see monthly pass/fail against those targets for reporting.
acceptanceCriteria: Add sla_targets table (user_id, api_id, uptime_target_pct, latency_p95_target_ms)
SLA report page shows target vs actual per month with pass/fail badge
Dashboard summary shows 'N/M SLA targets met this month'
Export SLA report as CSV (Pro+)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: sla_targets table in migration 020, /sla report page with pass/fail badges and CSV export, dashboard SLA summary widget in My Stack, incident-based uptime calculation
